title: (GATED) Patient-facing plain-language versions of the explainers
context: Patient-facing plain-language versions of the stats-for-clinicians explainers are explicitly out of scope for the current project. Any patient-facing rendering of statistical content is a separate high-risk deed requiring oncologist sign-off, patient-advocate sign-off, and a structural 'not medical advice' framing before any work may begin. This task entry exists in the backlog only as a gated placeholder to make the escalation boundary explicit and to prevent scope creep — it must not be worked until the high-risk prerequisites are fully satisfied. If this task is ever activated, a fresh deed must be scoped with oncologist and patient-advocate reviewers named, a 'not medical advice' frame required, and verifiedNeed confirmed with a patient-advocacy organization.
objective: GATED — do not start without satisfying all prerequisites. If activated: produce patient-facing plain-language versions of the stats-for-clinicians explainers, adapted for a lay audience, with oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off and a structural 'not medical advice' frame.
acceptanceCriteria: PREREQUISITE (must be satisfied before any work): a named, credentialed oncologist and a named patient-advocate reviewer have agreed to serve as sign-off reviewers for this task.
PREREQUISITE: a named patient-advocacy organization or patient-facing platform has confirmed it will adopt and use the patient-facing versions (verifiedNeed must be true before work starts).

PREREQUISITE: the scoping review has confirmed that no content will constitute medical advice, treatment recommendations, prognosis, or dosing guidance; the 'not medical advice' frame is structural (not a footnote) in every patient-facing document.

If all prerequisites are met: patient-facing versions are authored to a lay reading level (lower than the clinician/trainee versions) with simplified language, without sacrificing accuracy; a trained plain-language specialist reviews each version.
Oncologist sign-off and patient-advocate sign-off are both recorded before any patient-facing version is published.
No patient data is used; the statistical content is general methods education adapted for a lay audience, not patient-specific advice.
Biostatistician statsSignoff is also required (the accuracy gate remains non-skippable even for lay versions).
resources: PLAN.md — Scope (out of scope: patient-facing content); Quality gates (standing escalation to riskTier: high)
Elyos good-deed definition: riskTier:high requires credentialed expert sign-off before work begins
output: GATED. If prerequisites are met: patient-facing plain-language explainer versions, oncologist + patient-advocate signed-off, with structural 'not medical advice' frame. Until prerequisites are met: no output; this task must not be started.
